text: Oats ranked. where does this actually belong? Today we are ranking Oats as a grain, per 100 grams, so you can see where it really lands.
fat is 6.9 grams per 100 grams. omega 3 is absurdly strong. for a grain, fibre and glycemic behaviour do the heavy lifting.
carbs is 66.3 grams per 100 grams. the fibre support is genuinely impressive. for a grain, the whole fight is between stability and refinement.
protein is 16.9 grams per 100 grams. bioavailability is kind of absurd. for a grain, fibre and glycemic behaviour do the heavy lifting.
vitamin e at 3% DV stand out most. everything else is lackluster.
magnesium at 44% DV, iron at 26% DV stand out most.
biggest pros here. contains useful beta-glucans. easy to build meals around. can support digestive regularity.
biggest cons here. contains some anti-nutrients. healthiness depends on what you add to it. not ideal for everyone digestively.
Verdict: A tier. This food lands in A tier with an overall score of 65 from a base nutrition score of 62. Context lifted it by 3 points.
